# Head Judge

**Auth**: 6-character secret code (per round, generated by admin) → signed `rw_official_session` cookie
**Route prefix**: `/head-judge/events/[eventId]/...`
**Theme**: Dark

## What a head judge does

- Oversees the panel of judges during a round
- Confirms or overrides red cards before they count toward DQ
- Monitors the round activity log to spot anomalies

The head-judge workspace ([components/head-judge/head-judge-view.tsx](../../components/head-judge/head-judge-view.tsx)) surfaces only confirm/override actions — it does **not** present card-issuing buttons (that is first-line judging). Note that the card Server Actions *do* permit a `HEAD_JUDGE` position to issue cards, but that UI is not exposed in this workspace today. This may change — see [decisions/](../decisions/).

## Features used

| Feature | Notes |
|---------|-------|
| [secret-code-access](../features/secret-code-access.md) | Join via code → official cookie |
| [card-scoring](../features/card-scoring.md) | Confirm / override pending reds |
| [event-logging](../features/event-logging.md) | View round timeline |
| [live-scoreboard](../features/live-scoreboard.md) | Cross-reference public view |

## Pages visited

- [Join](../pages/head-judge/join.md)
- [Workspace](../pages/head-judge/workspace.md)

## How it works

After the secret-code join (`joinAsOfficial`, [app/actions/officials.ts](../../app/actions/officials.ts)), the workspace is a Server-Component read that auto-refreshes via `AutoRefresh` (`router.refresh()` every 2000ms while SCHEDULED, 2500ms otherwise). Confirm/Override call `confirmRedCard` / `rejectRedCard` in [app/actions/cards.ts](../../app/actions/cards.ts) — both require the `HEAD_JUDGE` position and an `ONGOING` round, and are atomic/idempotent (`PENDING → CONFIRMED | OVERRIDDEN`). On a confirm that brings an athlete to 4 confirmed reds, the action sets the athlete `DQ`; if that empties the field it auto-ends the round.

## Typical workflow

1. Receive code from admin; open `/head-judge/events/[eventId]/join`.
2. Enter the 6-char code → land on workspace.
3. Watch the athlete table and activity log as judges issue cards.
4. When a red card needs review, click Confirm or Override.
5. After the round, the workspace becomes read-only history.
